title: Tangy Toasted Canadian Cream Cheese Flatbread
description: A garlicky and molten Canadian dish featuring cream cheese on long-fermentation-dough, perfect for a low-sodium diet, served in a bento-box. This energizing flatbread is great for a quick snack or lunch.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Roll out the long-fermentation-dough to a thickness of 1/4 inch.
Transfer the dough to a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Spread the softened cream cheese evenly over the dough, leaving a 1/2 inch border around the edges.
Sprinkle the minced garlic, salt, black pepper, and dried parsley over the cream cheese.
Drizzle the olive oil and lemon juice over the top of the flatbread.
Bake the flatbread in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown and the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Remove the flatbread from the oven and let it cool for 5 minutes before slicing and serving.
Serve the flatbread in a bento-box for a convenient and energizing snack or lunch.
